title: Memory Maneuver
description: Parse a tree encoded as a flat number sequence, then sum all metadata entries and compute the root node's value under an index-reference rule.
writeup:
## Approach

The input is a single flat list of integers that encodes a tree. Each node starts with two header numbers (child count, metadata count), followed by its children recursively, then its metadata entries.

## Part 1

Recursively parse the flat list and sum every metadata entry in the entire tree:

- `parse(numbers, pos)` reads the header, recurses into each child, then slices out the metadata.
- `sum_meta(node)` sums the node's own metadata plus the recursive sum over all children.

One clean recursive pass handles both parsing and summation.

## Part 2

The value of a node depends on whether it has children:

- **No children** → value is the sum of its metadata entries (same as Part 1).
- **Has children** → metadata entries are 1-based indexes into the child list. Value is the sum of the values of the referenced children (invalid indexes are skipped).

`node_value(node)` is a straightforward recursion using a list comprehension to filter valid indexes.

## Code Highlights

- `parse` returns both the structured node and the updated position, keeping the logic stateless.
- Part 2 reuses the already-parsed tree — no re-parsing.
- Visualizer animates the DFS traversal node-by-node, then walks the value computation top-down for the first few levels to show how the index-reference rule propagates.
